html:
Exclusive to:  

The Row Boat is a Boat that can be crafted from the Nautical Tab in the Shipwrecked DLC. It requires a Science Machine to prototype as well as 3 Boards and 4 Vines to craft. It has 250 durability and can be fitted with boat attachments. If it takes damage, it can be repaired using a Boat Repair Kit or the Sea Yard.

While moving, it loses durability at a rate of 1 per 5.76 seconds, lasting a total of 3 Days, arguably making it the most efficient boat for traveling.

Occasionally, abandoned Row Boats can be found floating in Medium or Deep Ocean, usually equipped with a low durability Sail and a Boat Lantern.

Trivia

  • The crafting description is a reference to the English song of the same name.
  • Wx78's quote is a reference to the 2004 film I, Robot.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

It has 250 durability and can be fitted with boat attachments. If it takes damage, it can be repaired using a Boat Repair Kit or the Sea Yard.
While moving, it loses durability at a rate of 1 per 5.76 seconds, lasting a total of 3 Days, arguably making it the most efficient boat for traveling.
Occasionally, abandoned Row Boats can be found floating in Medium or Deep Ocean, usually equipped with a low durability Sail and a Boat Lantern.

The '''Row Boat''' is a [[Boats|Boat]] that can be crafted from the [[Nautical Tab]] in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. It requires a [[Science Machine]] to prototype as well as 3 [[Boards]] and 4 [[Vine]]s to craft. It has 250 durability and can be fitted with boat attachments. If it takes damage, it can be repaired using a [[Boat Repair Kit]] or the [[Sea Yard]].

While moving, it loses durability at a rate of 1 per 5.76 seconds, lasting a total of 3 Days, arguably making it the most efficient boat for traveling.

Occasionally, abandoned Row Boats can be found floating in Medium or Deep [[Ocean]], usually equipped with a low durability [[Sail]] and a [[Boat Lantern]].

== {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The crafting description is a reference to the English song of the same name.
* Wx78's quote is a reference to the 2004 film ''I, Robot''.
